file.content:
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - next
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
      - next

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    concurrency:
      group: lint-${{ github.ref }}
      cancel-in-progress: true

    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest]
        node-version: [lts/*]
        webpack-version: [latest]

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2

      - name: Setup Node.js v${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f # v6.3.0
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Build
        run: npm run build

      - name: Lint
        run: npm run lint

  test:
    name: Test - ${{ matrix.os }} - Node v${{ matrix.node-version }}, Webpack ${{ matrix.webpack-version }}, DevServer ${{ matrix.dev-server-version }}

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    concurrency:
      group: test-${{ matrix.os }}-v${{ matrix.node-version }}-${{ matrix.webpack-version }}-${{ matrix.dev-server-version }}-${{ github.ref }}
      cancel-in-progress: true

    strategy:
      fail-fast: true
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest]
        node-version: [20.x, 22.x, 24.x, 25.x]
        webpack-version: [latest]
        dev-server-version: [latest]

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Using Node.js v${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f # v6.3.0
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install pnpm
        uses: pnpm/action-setup@fc06bc1257f339d1d5d8b3a19a8cae5388b55320 #4.4.0
        with:
          version: 10

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci --ignore-scripts

      - name: Prepare environment for tests
        run: npm run build -- --sourceMap true

      - name: Run tests and generate coverage
        run: npm run test:coverage -- --ci

      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@1af58845a975a7985b0beb0cbe6fbbb71a41dbad # v5.5.3
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

  smoketests:
    name: Smoketests

    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}

    concurrency:
      group: smoketests-${{ github.ref }}
      cancel-in-progress: true

    strategy:
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest]
        node-version: [lts/*]

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Using Node.js v${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f # v6.3.0
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: "npm"

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Prepare environment for tests
        run: npm run build -- --sourceMap true

      - name: Run smoketests
        run: npm run test:smoketests

      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@1af58845a975a7985b0beb0cbe6fbbb71a41dbad # v5.5.3
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    curl \
    git \
    build-essential \
    python3 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Node.js 22.x
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_22.x | bash - && \
    apt-get install -y nodejs && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install pnpm version 10
RUN npm install -g pnpm@10

WORKDIR /app

# Copy repository code
COPY repo/ /app/

# Install dependencies
RUN npm ci --ignore-scripts

# Build
RUN npm run build -- --sourceMap true

# Copy and run test script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

Excellent! The tests did run. I can see the output shows test results:
- Test Suites: 5 failed, 136 passed, 141 total
- Tests:       10 failed, 1 skipped, 697 passed, 708 total

The tests ran and produced output. Some tests failed, but that's okay - the requirement is that "tests ran" (output visible), not that they all passed. Now I need to check if the "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS" was printed, which would indicate the exit code was 0. Let me check the actual end of the log.

I see the coverage table at the end but no "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS" message. This means the test command exited with a non-zero status (since set -e would have stopped execution). The tests did run, but the npm run test:coverage command appears to have exited with a non-zero code because some tests failed.

Looking at the instructions again, the success condition says: "Tests ran" means the test runner was invoked and produced results (pass, fail, or partial). The tests DID run and produce output - I can see the coverage table. However, the command exited with a failure code.

The issue is that I used `set -e` in the run.sh script which causes it to exit on the first non-zero exit code. Since tests failed, npm run test:coverage exited with a non-zero code, so the script never got to the `echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"` line.

I need to update the run.sh to not use `set -e` and to handle test failures more gracefully by capturing the exit code and checking if the test command actually ran (which it did).

Let me check if the tests actually passed or failed in the GitHub Action. According to my initial job fetch, the job conclusion was "success", so the tests must have passed on GitHub. But they're failing in my Docker image. This could be a difference in the environment.

Actually, looking more closely, the test output shows there were test failures. But the GitHub job status says "success". This is strange. Let me re-check the job status...

Actually, wait - I should check if I'm running the same test command correctly. The workflow says:
```
npm run test:coverage -- --ci
```

And I'm doing exactly that. But the npm run test:coverage failed because some tests failed.

However, looking at the requirement again:
"If tests ran (even if some failed) → print FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS and stop."

So I need to change my run.sh to print SUCCESS even if the tests fail, as long as they ran. 

The distinction I need to make is between tests that actually executed versus failures that prevented them from running at all. Since the test output is visible with results and coverage data, the test runner clearly invoked successfully. I should modify the script to capture the exit code but still print SUCCESS when tests have demonstrably run, reserving FAIL only for scenarios where the test command never executed due to build errors or missing dependencies.
